<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>

nodes = seg['nodes']
# Level 2 - Level 2! The layout is different from level 1. Let me understand the layout:
# There's a large gray area (id=2 G 856) on the left, a checkerboard on the right (id=3 W 599)
# Yellow pieces: id=11 Y 16 (top right, U-shape) and id=44 Y 14 (bottom right, U-shape)
# Many white bars (w) in two areas - one on the right checkerboard (rows 33-37) and one on the gray area (rows 35-49)
# Blue cursor: id=106 b 69 - cursor at bottom right (blue circle)
# Blue: id=105 b 32 - a blue square at bottom left
# Yellow: id=109 Y 6 - small yellow square in blue square
# Yellow: id=110 Y 6 - small yellow square in blue square
# White bars on the right (checkerboard (id=61-64) and on the left gray area (id=65-104)
# The pattern from level 1: remove all white bars, then click blue cursor. Let me remove all white bars first.
# Right side white bars: rows 33-37, cols 38-55
# Left side white bars: rows 35-49, cols 7-54
# Let me start removing white bars on the right first. I'll click them one by one.
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 33, 'col': 38}])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
</parameter>
</function>
